metadata.title: States PCS: Maharashtra GK Questions for MPSC Examinations

questions.question: Which traditional saree is Aurangabad in Maharashtra especially famous for?
questions.explanation: Paithani sarees from Aurangabad, Maharashtra, are celebrated for vibrant handwoven silk, rich gold and silver zari, and intricate designs. These sarees represent Maharashtra’s textile heritage and are often worn at festivals and weddings.

questions.question: Consider the following statements regarding the foundation of the Indian National Congress: The Indian National Congress was founded in Bombay in 1885. The first session was held at Gokuldas Tejpal Sanskrit College. The first session was attended by prominent leaders like Dadabhai Naoroji and W.C. Bonnerjee. Which of the above statements is/are correct?
questions.explanation: Statement 1 is correct: The Indian National Congress (INC) was indeed founded in Bombay in 1885. Statement 2 is correct: The first session was held at Gokuldas Tejpal Sanskrit College, Bombay. Statement 3 is incorrect: While W.C. Bonnerjee presided, Dadabhai Naoroji did not attend the first session, as he was in England. Hence, option 1 is correct.

questions.question: In which year did Sangli raisins from Maharashtra become the first raisin variety in India to receive the Geographical Indication (GI) tag?
questions.explanation: Sangli raisins from Maharashtra received the GI tag on May 18, 2016, becoming the first raisin variety in India to achieve this status. The recognition protects the product’s identity and authenticity, and was proposed by the Maharashtra Rajya Draksh Bagaitdar Sangh.

questions.question: Who among the following Maratha rulers built the Kolaba Fort located in Alibaug, Maharashtra?
questions.explanation: Kolaba Fort was constructed by Chhatrapati Shivaji Maharaj in 1680 on the coast of Alibaug, Maharashtra. The fort served as a strategic naval base for the Maratha Empire and is one of the prominent sea forts on the Konkan coast.

questions.question: Which European colonial power built Fort George in the Fort area of Mumbai in 1769?
questions.explanation: Fort George in Mumbai was constructed by the British in 1769, named after King George III. It served as the administrative center of the Bombay Presidency and helped secure British control following the removal of Dongri Hill, a previous stronghold for Marathi forces.

questions.question: Which group of brothers from Maharashtra gained prominence in 1897 for assassinating British officials as an act of resistance against colonial rule during the bubonic plague in Pune?
questions.explanation: The Chapekar brothers—Damodar, Vasudev, and Balkrishna—from Maharashtra assassinated British officers in 1897 in Pune, protesting oppressive colonial actions during the plague. Their act marked one of the earliest instances of armed resistance against British rule in India’s freedom movement.

questions.question: Who authored Saurabhasya, the well-known commentary on Suryasidhanta, and was a prominent scholar from the Golagrama lineage in Maharashtra?
questions.explanation: Nṛsiṃha Gaṇaka, born in 1586 in Maharashtra’s Golagrama lineage, wrote Saurabhasya, a commentary on Suryasidhanta. He made notable contributions to astronomy and mathematics, enhancing Golagrama’s stature as an astronomical center in Maharashtra.

questions.question: Who constructed the Asheri Fort located in Palghar district, Maharashtra?
questions.explanation: Asheri Fort was built around 800 years ago by Bhojraja of the Shilahara dynasty. The fort is near Khodkona in Palghar, Maharashtra. It changed hands among the Kolis, Bimb Raja of Mahim, Gujarat Sultanate, Portuguese, Marathas, and British, reflecting a diverse historical legacy.

questions.question: Which Marathi social activist burned the Manusmriti during the Mahad Satyagraha?
questions.explanation: Gangadhar Nilkanth Sahasrabuddhe, a Marathi Chitpawan Brahmin social activist, burned Manusmriti during the 1927 Mahad Satyagraha led by Dr. Ambedkar to protest caste discrimination. He also edited Ambedkar’s weekly publication ‘Janata’ and was closely associated with other reformers from Maharashtra.

questions.question: Which dynasty founded by Malik Raja Farooqui ruled Dhule region?
questions.explanation: Malik Raja Farooqui founded the Farooqi dynasty in 1370. It ruled Khandesh, including Dhule, until the Mughal annexation. Dhule was part of the historical Khandesh region, and the Farooqi dynasty’s rulers played a key role in its medieval political landscape.
